threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep trying to order a pint after 10 PM and getting confused stares from the bar staff. Welcome to Hackney's late-night scene!
Caught the 37 last night, a guy loudly arguing with a seat about how they both deserve better lives. Guess the night brings out the philosophers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old mattress as a trampoline in the park, laughing as if it were the best day ever—pure Neukölln spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble on a rooftop cinema screening old films while the sun sets over the Spree. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they order their burrito—no rice, extra salsa, and always a Coke. Tourists go for the full burrito, every time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 late night on Valencia, and the driver blasted classic jazz. A mini concert while cruising past taco trucks felt like a slice of SF magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares, but the real vibe is in the narrow streets at night, where locals gather for secret concerts.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro maps; they still ask locals for directions to places that are just around the corner.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road when the light's red, not realizing half of us don't bother. It's all about survival of the fittest here.
Caught a guy on the 59 last night trying to convince a pigeon to join him for a pint. Pigeon didn’t budge, but the whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The evening air thick with shisha smoke, kids skating past the kebab shop—when I heard the call to prayer mixing with techno from a nearby party, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a youth club that doubles as a makeshift art gallery, where local teens curate their own exhibitions alongside graffiti artists.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You spot a true local if they can name the taco truck at 24th and Mission before you even ask. Those who just got here can’t tell the difference.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 Mission with a guy playing a ukulele, belting out oldies while the rest of us just sat in traffic. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street performer is a genius, but most are just rehashed covers from a Madrid bar. The real talent is often busking in the shadows.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: New arrivals always look a bit confused at the L10 Sud, especially during rush hour. You can spot them scanning the signs for direction while the locals know to follow their noses.
